---
title: Diction and Register
tags: [diction, style]
---

# Diction and Register

Diction is word choice at the sentence level: plain versus ornate,
concrete versus abstract, contemporary versus archaic. A voice record
should name a diction target precisely enough that two different
writing passes converge on the same register — "evocative but not
purple" is not precise; "concrete sensory nouns, short sentences,
almost no adverbs" is.

## Banned words as a diction tool

A short banned-word list is a cheap, effective way to hold diction
steady: naming the crutch words a model reaches for by default (rather
sublime tapestry, palpable) forces more specific choices in every
passage that would otherwise have used them.

## Register drift under pressure

Long fight or chase passages are where diction most often drifts
toward generic action-movie phrasing. Hold the same concrete,
sensory-detail standard in a fast scene as in a slow one.
